faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Oakwood homes?
faqs.answer: Oakwood residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, cigarette beetles, flour beetles, and weevils. These pests typically arrive in packaged foods from grocery stores and quickly spread throughout pantries and cabinets. Our technicians conduct thorough inspections to identify all affected food items and nesting areas before treatment.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a pantry pest problem in Oakwood?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Oakwood and surrounding Staten Island neighborhoods. Most pantry pest treatments can be completed in a single visit lasting 1-2 hours, depending on the severity of the infestation and the size of your kitchen and storage areas.

faqs.question: What does the $350 - $550 pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed inspection of all food storage areas, identification of infested items, targeted treatment of affected cabinets and shelves, application of safe pest control products, and prevention recommendations. Pricing varies based on home size, infestation severity, and whether follow-up treatments are needed.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: After treatment, we recommend storing dry goods in airtight containers, regularly cleaning shelves and cabinets, inspecting groceries before storing them, and rotating older items to the front. We provide detailed prevention guidelines specific to your Oakwood home and offer follow-up inspections to ensure long-term protection.
